methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 45.3x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Overview Energy is a Seed-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Marc Berte that has raised $20M to date. The valuation universe comprises five public comps (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) with a median P/S of 45.3x and eight private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, and Blue Origin.

Only the Stage-Progression method is applicable here. Using total capital raised ($20M) as a proxy for current valuation and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $50M. The Peer-Median approach cannot be applied due to insufficient narrow-filtered peers in the Seed stage within Space & Aerospace (zero same-stage matches, one adjacent-stage, zero similar-tier comps). Public-Comp Anchoring is inapplicable because Overview Energy's revenue is not on file, precluding P/S multiples from meaningful application.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $42M–$57M, centered at $50M. This estimate carries material uncertainty: it relies on a single methodological input (stage-progression multiplier) and lacks revenue visibility needed to cross-check against public market multiples or narrow private peer sets. The wide band reflects this single-method limitation rather than convergence across approaches. Key risks to valuation include technology de-risking trajectory, capital efficiency relative to sector peers, and path to revenue generation.
